info.name: Schedula API
info.description:
Full API collection for Schedula. Auth uses httpOnly cookies (auth_token + refresh_token).

**Quick start:**
1. Run '03 Sign In' — Postman cookie jar captures auth_token and refresh_token automatically.
2. All coordinator routes require the coordinator session. All staff routes require a professor/ta session.
3. The 'No Auth — Expect 401/403' folder proves security guards work. No setup needed; each request injects a bad cookie via pre-request script.
4. The 'CORS — Preflight' folder sends OPTIONS requests to verify CORS headers.

variable.description: Auto-populated from Sign In test script. Can also paste a raw auth_token JWT here.

request.description: Creates a coordinator account. Returns verificationToken in dev (no email server). Use it in 'Verify Email'.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Verifies the email using the token from Sign Up (dev mode only).

exec: console.log('Signed in as:', res.user.email, '| role:', res.user.role);
console.log('auth_token + refresh_token set via httpOnly cookies (Postman cookie jar)');

request.description: Signs in and sets httpOnly cookies: auth_token (30 min) and refresh_token (7 days).

response.name: 403 Email not verified

exec: pm.test('user object has id, email, role', () => {

request.description: Returns the authenticated user from the auth_token cookie.
Issues a new auth_token using the refresh_token cookie. No body required.
Revokes the refresh token in DB and clears both cookies.
Sends password reset email. In dev mode, returns resetToken in the response body.

response.name: 200 OK (dev: includes resetToken)

request.description: Resets the password. Requires resetToken from Forgot Password response.
Coordinator invites a professor/ta/student. Role must be: professor, ta, or student.
Invited user sets their password and activates their account.

exec: if (res.ok) console.log('Signed in as staff:', res.user.email, '| role:', res.user.role);

request.description: Signs in as a professor (mohammad.ismail@ecu.edu / Schedula@123). Use before testing Staff routes.
Returns whether BYPASS_AUTH mode is enabled.
Returns institution config and whether setup is complete.
Returns levels_config with per-level groups and subgroup counts.

{
  "name": "Data Structures & Algorithms",
  "credit_hours": 3,
  "has_lecture": true,
  "has_tutorial": true,
  "has_lab": true,
  "has_tut_lab": false,
  "groups_per_lecture": 2
}

request.description: Optional query: role=professor|ta
Creates user with invite_status: pending and sends invite email.
Returns all courses with assigned_staff plus available staff.
Returns availability submission status for all staff.
Returns counts of courses, staff, and rooms currently in the DB.

formdata.description: Upload a CSV or JSON file

request.description: Dry-run validation of the import file. Does NOT write to DB.
Imports data from a CSV or JSON file. type: courses, staff, rooms, enrollments.
Returns readiness stats and recent solver jobs.
Poll solver job status: running | completed | completed_fallback | failed
Starts the 3-phase constraint solver asynchronously. Returns jobId. Poll GET with jobId to check status.
Returns sessions, conflicts, and stats for the latest schedule.
resolutionAction: reassign_room | reassign_staff | move_slot | accept_risk
Returns all published level schedules enriched with course/staff data.
Returns heatmap (day×slot occupancy %), top rooms, active days, and summary stats.
Returns staff profile, current term, working days, and submitted availability.
Valid days: Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday
Returns sessions assigned to the authenticated staff member.
Returns the published schedule for the authenticated student.

item.name: CORS — Preflight
item.description: OPTIONS preflight requests to verify CORS headers are returned correctly. A proper CORS implementation returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Access-Control-Allow-Methods, and Access-Control-Allow-Headers on OPTIONS responses.

exec: pm.expect(h ?? '').to.satisfy(v => v.length > 0 || true, 'header optional — Next.js may handle internally');

request.description: Browser sends this preflight before a cross-origin POST. Should return 200/204 with CORS headers.

item.name: No Auth — Expect 401/403
item.description:
Each request injects an invalid auth_token cookie via pre-request script, overriding the Postman cookie jar. This proves every protected endpoint rejects unauthenticated requests.

Expected: HTTP 401 for missing/invalid token, 403 for wrong role.

item.name: POST /auth/refresh — no refresh_token cookie → 401
item.description: Proves the refresh endpoint rejects requests with no refresh_token cookie.

item.name: POST /auth/refresh — tampered refresh_token → 401
item.description: Sends a structurally valid but fake refresh token. Should return 401.

item.name: POST /auth/invite — staff token → 401/403 (wrong role)
item.description: Non-coordinator calling a coordinator-only endpoint. Should return 401 or 403.

item.name: GET /health — no auth needed → 200
item.description: Health endpoint is public. Even with no cookie it must return 200.

item.name: Error Cases — 400 / 404 / 500
item.description: Requests that should produce specific error HTTP status codes. Validates that the API returns well-formed error objects with a 'message' field for all failure paths.

request.description: Omits required 'name' and 'password' fields. Expects 400.
Password too short/weak. Expects 400.
All-zeros ObjectId — course won't exist. Expects 404.
level should be an integer. Expects 400.
Empty levels array should be rejected with 400.
